The current donor eligibility assessment process at our centers faces several challenges, leading to inefficiencies, potential errors, and delays. title: Interface for epidemiological data relevant to Signal Assessment

intake.raw_summary: It takes a long period of time to obtain relevant epidemiological data relevant to a signal assessment.  Finding the background rate, for example, can come from several different sources, from all articles.  This takes time to review.  For example, what is the % of hypersensitivity seen in adult patients.

intake.raw_summary:
•	Problem: In the pharmaceutical industry, managing RFPs/RFIs is often a time-consuming and resource-intensive process. The sheer volume of data and the need for comprehensive analysis can lead to inefficiencies, potential errors, and delays in decision-making.
•	Opportunity: Leveraging Generative AI (GenAI) can streamline the RFP/RFI management process, create consistency and systematic approach. Automating certain tasks would enhance the overall efficiency of reviewing and comparing responses.

structured.problem_statement:
•	Problem: In the pharmaceutical industry, managing RFPs/RFIs is often a time-consuming and resource-intensive process. The sheer volume of data and the need for comprehensive analysis can lead to inefficiencies, potential errors, and delays in decision-making.
•	Opportunity: Leveraging Generative AI (GenAI) can streamline the RFP/RFI management process, create consistency and systematic approach.

intake.raw_summary: Review of provision / collection of Essential documents for site activation and identifying items inadequate or incomplete (e.g. PI name not specified by SI on a FDF; instructions not removed from the standard form before site completes - i.e. the simple and obvious errors identified and reported to the team to address on an ongoing basis prior to final review by CSA / COM / LCSA/LCOM.

intake.raw_summary: Create bot to verify training completion for the team managing user access in eCAM.  Would impact CDIT and ET teams.  Idea submitted by Jen Arters during CDIT workshop.

structured.problem_statement: Create bot to verify training completion for the team managing user access in eCAM.  Would impact CDIT and ET teams.  Idea submitted by Jen Arters during CDIT workshop.

title: Gen AI to prospectively identify Regulatory Inspection risks in clinical trials

intake.raw_summary: Using previously know Reg inspection findings (from CSL or publicly known findings), as well as common areas Inspectors are know for digging into: search these against our study documents, study data, procedural documents, TMF, systems, etc. (anything subject to inspection) to uncover issues, concerns, risks that are typically found by a human inspector.

title: AI in Medical Coding and Clinical Data Reconciliations

intake.raw_summary: These two ideas have been already submitted during the Cut the Crap challenge (see attachments). The Medical Coding idea didn't get any attention back then and the AI in reconciliations idea won the challenge but the progress of the inititative is very slow. I discussed with Steve to resubmit the ideas.

title: Use AI to review signal patterns for future protocol designs

intake.raw_summary: AI in my understanding has the capacity of look at wide range of data patterns and identify areas where majority of risks that were identified during Risk assessment were converted to issues. A meaningful model once created can helps us understand the areas of risk and use the identified patterns to identify pitfalls during study outline stages and aid less risky protocol designs.

A meaningful model once created can helps us understand the areas of risk and use the identified patterns to identify pitfalls during study outline stages and aid less risky protocol designs.

title: No Code Biomedical and Bioinformatics Research Platform - To effectively and efficiently extract insights to inform target and indication selection

intake.raw_summary: Extracting insights from the massive amount of literature and molecular biomedical data sources is time-consuming and biased procedure and requires specialist bioinformatics and computational expertise to inform asset prioritization and indication prioritization.

structured.problem_statement: Extracting insights from the massive amount of literature and molecular biomedical data sources is time-consuming and biased procedure and requires specialist bioinformatics and computational expertise to inform asset prioritization and indication prioritization.

title: Plasma Protein Research Intelligence - Focus on circulating plasma proteins will generate insights across TAs and platforms

intake.raw_summary:
Identifying and understanding plasma protein potential to modify disease requires manual literature searches and data mining focused on one protein at a time. This process:
Is biased and prone to missing relevant data.
Requires high FTE resources investment.
Is not scalable to 1000s of plasma proteins and all TA targeted indications.

structured.problem_statement:
Identifying and understanding plasma protein potential to modify disease requires manual literature searches and data mining focused on one protein at a time. This process:
Is biased and prone to missing relevant data.
Requires high FTE resources investment.
Is not scalable to 1000s of plasma proteins and all TA targeted indications.

title: Auto-report generation to accelerate drug development - Consistent report generation and drafting to reduce project cycle times

intake.raw_summary:
Preparation of SOPs, WI, validation protocols, validation reports, and bioanalysis reports is time inefficient and typically requires significant time from senior members of RCB (from document and content creation, to review and approval). 
Although data standardization and templates reduces the time burden, preparation of documents is often a bottleneck in leading to some increases in cycle times. 
Medical writers have been used to alleviate this but budget restrictions restrict significant use

title: Auto-Feedback Generation for New Product Opportunities - Consolidating and drafting internal reviewers’ feedback on external opportunities

intake.raw_summary: Providing thoughtful and constructive feedback on external opportunities has been highly effective for differentiating CSL as a ‘partner of a choice’. The REI team consolidates feedback from multiple internal reviewers to provide guidance on e.g. key data gaps, and encourage re-engagement as a project progresses. title: Monitor and Analyze Reported Adverse Events

intake.raw_summary: Real-time monitoring: AI algorithms could  continuously analyze real-time patient data to detect safety signals and  identify adverse events, This can help ensure patient safety and enable early intervention if necessary.

structured.problem_statement: Real-time monitoring: AI algorithms could  continuously analyze real-time patient data to detect safety signals and  identify adverse events, This can help ensure patient safety and enable early intervention if necessary.

intake.raw_summary: CSL is committed to and invests in collecting patient feedback throughout the lifecycle. A Patient Insights GPT would allow employees to make plain language queries about patient preferences and lived experience and receive contextualized answers from CSL’s collection of patient insights. This can inform operational plans, clinical protocols and more. Currently, the feedback received is cataloged in summarizing documents and transcripts then stored for future reference.

title: Brainstorm, generate, or update clinical development plans

intake.raw_summary: A well constructed clinical development plan can improve the probability of success, lessen time to market through both increased speed of development and decreasing the white space between trials.  Most importantly, it can help us make better decisions to proceed, halt or slow development.  Further, the creation of a well thought out CDP takes time.
